_metadata.description: Tironian notes and medieval Latin abbreviations inventory

tironian_et.note: The only universally surviving Tironian sign in 14th-15th c. manuscripts

nasal_bar.note: Most common abbreviation device — appears on virtually every line

contraction.description: Key letters retained (first+last), bar over omission

superscript_vowels.description: Small letter above replaces intervening letters

planetary_symbols.description: Used in alchemical-medical texts for metals/substances

veneto_notes.description: Conventions specific to Veneto scriptoria (14th-15th c.)
characteristics: Transition period: Gothic cursive → humanistic minuscule
Standard Latin abbreviation marks apply with regional idiosyncrasies
Heavy use of suspension in medical/herbal texts
Ligatures for common syllables (st, ct, et)
references: Derolez, 'The Palaeography of Gothic Manuscript Books' (2003)
Archivio di Stato di Venezia collections
